title: Slugs, Permalinks, Redirects, Canonicals, and Route Manifests · Program Defense
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for slugs, permalinks, redirects, canonicals, and route manifests and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, filesystem, parser, template, validator, host, CDN, DNS, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for collision-free versioned route and redirect manifest. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal…
objectives: Separate stable content identity from mutable title, source path, output path, display label, and public URL.
Define Unicode normalization, transliteration or preservation, case, separators, reserved names, empties, length, and collision behavior.
Select explicit or derived permalinks, trailing-slash convention, date behavior, language prefix, and base path with migration evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

Build deterministic Python evidence to produce stable collision-free routes, canonicals, and an acyclic redirect graph; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate stable content identity from mutable title, source path, output path, display label, and public URL.

content.value: Python urllib.parse Documentation — URL splitting, joining, quoting, relative resolution, security caveats, and RFC versus WHATWG behavior.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Canonical Origin, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Canonical Origin precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Canonical Origin, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A quiz publishing team handles case 7054 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to produce stable collision-free routes, canonicals, and an acyclic redirect graph; retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Build canonical, alternate-language, feed, and sitemap URLs only from production origin and route evidence, never request or preview state.

For Static-site publishing and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Canonical Origin, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: A relative canonical link is portable and unambiguous.

stimulus.title: Canonical Origin failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A relative canonical link is portable and unambiguous.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build canonical, alternate-language, feed, and sitemap URLs only from production origin and route evidence, never request or preview state.

options.text: Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority. That claim does not survive the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary.

For diagnose the hidden failure, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof.
Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Canonical Origin
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Canonical Origin: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz publishing team handles case 8673 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to produce stable collision-free routes, canonicals, and an acyclic redirect graph; retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Build canonical, alternate-language, feed, and sitemap URLs only from production origin and route evidence, never request or preview state.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Canonical Origin.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Canonical Origin, keep Canonical Origin connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Build canonical, alternate-language, feed, and sitemap URLs only from production origin and route evidence, never request or preview state.
